Description: Le Port de Cotonou, port en eau profonde. Superficie : Plan d’eau 400.000 m², superficie totale 60 ha. Deux digues abritent le plan d’eau. Le port comprend : au nord : 6 postes conventionnels de 155 à 180 m chacun, 1 poste à conteneurs de 200 ml, 1 poste roulier. Jeté Est : un poste pétrolier et pondéreux de 200 ml, un poste de 160 ml pour le chargement des huiles végétales, 1 poste de 100 ml pour les chalutiers et les navires de faible tonnage. Sud de la darse : un poste pétroliers et gaziers de 250 ml à -11 m hydro. Sur financement du programme du Millenium Challenge Corporation des  Etats-Unis, il est construit un nouveau quai (2 postes) à conteneurs de 600 ml environ à -15 m hydro et un parking de 50 000 m² pour 250 camions et est installé un système de vidéo surveillance du port. Par ailleurs, il existe au port de nombreux magasins, entrepôts de transit, parcs à conteneurs, terres pleins bitumés et stations de sapeurs pompier. Il comprend cinq  IP et quatre ZAR.

L'IP est implantée dans le Port  de Cotonou. Elle est dédiée à l’accostage et à la manutention des porte-conteneurs. L’IP comprend principalement : -Un quai de 515 ml composé de 3 postes d’accostage et d’une rampe ; -Le magasin N°5 (Jonction entre l’IP 1 et l’IP 2) dans sa partie Est ; -Des zones de stockage en périphérie de l’IP dans ses parties Nord & Ouest ; -4 accès principaux possibles (dont 2 avec PIF) et 4 accès secondaires (3 accès aux annexes « aires de stockage de conteneurs » Nord et Ouest et 1 accès mobile sur la zone quai entre IP 1 et IP 2) ; -Des moyens de manutention adaptés aux mouvements de conteneurs ; -Des moyens de vidéosurveillance et d’éclairage ; -Des ZAR en cours de transformation à la suite de l’ESP ; -Cette IP a trois annexes situées à l’extérieur du terminal, au Nord pour les parcs à conteneurs de Coman et Sobemap et à l’Ouest pour le parc à conteneurs de APM Terminal, avec également des bureaux (Data center de l’opérateur de l’IP)) et d’une Sub station

Cette IP est implantée dans le Port de Cotonou, Elle est dédiée à l’accostage et à la manutention des porte-conteneurs.-Un quai de 546 ml composé de 2 postes d’accostage ;-Un bâtiment administratif de direction d’exploitation hébergeant le serveur informatique de BT ;-Un atelier de maintenance ;-Une zone de stockage de conteneurs dans l’IP avec deux zones particulières dédiées Reefer et MD ;-4 accès possibles dont certains sont des PIF ;-Des moyens de manutention adaptés aux conteneurs ;-Un PCS avec des moyens de vidéosurveillance propre à BT ;-Des moyens d’éclairage ;-Huit  ZAR ;-Deux annexes (Base SMTC et le Zoning) à cette IP situées à l’extérieur du terminal.

L'IP est implantée dans le Port  de Cotonou et est dédiée à l’accostage et à la manutention des navires de vracs. The Palmeira Port in Sal Island, located in Palmeira Bay, is the third port in Cape Verde in terms of moving of cargo.It is composed with a single pier with 120 meters alongside (variable draft between 2,5 to 6 meters), and 1 group of mooring buoys to unload petroleum tankers. It is included in this port the following spaces and building:Pier, Small containers park, 2 stores, Workshop, Administrative building.

The Porto Grande in S. Vicente Island is a oceanic port, with sand bottoms, with a draft variable between 3,5 and 30 meters, with good natural conditions to make it available to easy and safe entrance and departure. The Port occupies a land area on the NEcoast of the Island of São Vicente. Multi purpose berth supports Container, Bulk, Tanker, LPG, General Cargo, Ferry and Cruise operations. The main port allows 1,173 meters to ship alongside perimeter and the cabotage terminal with 235 meters.

The Praia Port in Santiago Island is an oceanic port, with sand bottoms, with small dynamic variation, allowing the vessels an easy and safe entrance, departure and manoeuvring.The Port has alongside pier with a “L” shape, with about 690 metres of perimeter with draft between 7,5 and 9,5 meters.A multi purpose berth supports Container, Bulk, Tanker, LPG, General Cargo, Ferry and Cruise operations. All cargoes other than Oil, LPG and cement are loaded/unloaded using onboard cranes and equipment.  The port has a twenty-four hour capability, Piers nr 1 and 2, Containers park, A,B,C and D stores, Workshops, Administrative building, Cement Cylinders.

The Royal Dutch / Shell Group of Companies ["Shell"] has developed a Liquefied Natural Gas ["LNG"] receiving terminal at Hazira, Gujarat. The port development plan includes the long-term port development with non-LNG cargo terminals (container and bulk terminals). Construction of an LNG import terminal in the new port at Hazira started in April 2002. The LNG terminal will initially handle about 2.0 Mtpa (Phase 1A), expanding to a long-term throughput of 10.0 Mtpa. Studies are ongoing for the development of non-LNG cargo terminals in the port (Phase II). The LNG terminal is situated in the inter-tidal zone directly west of the forest boundary line. Number of LNG berths:	-  1, Minimum depth on berth-  13 m CD. Approach channel: An entrance channel connects the port with the deep water of the Sutherland Channel. The dredged depth of minus 12 meter CD allows for arrival and departure of LNG carriers up to a draft of 11.5 m.  The straight approach channel has a clear width of 400 m at the seaside tapering off to a width of 300 meter between the breakwaters to allow unobstructed easy 300 meter between the breakwaters to allow unobstructed easyentrance/departure of ships. The orientation of the approach channel isheading 070 - 250 degrees north.  LNG jetty General description: The berth of the LNG jetty is orientated 225 degrees north to minimize the anticipated downtime as a result of the combination of wind, waves and swell. The jetty consists of:  ·	Four breasting and five mooring dolphins fitted with fenders and quick release hooks and accessible by catwalks. ·	An unloading platform with a concrete deck providing support for piping and equipment. ·	A trestle to shore accommodating piping, cables and a roadway for personnel access and small vehicles. ·	The LNG jetty is equipped with a mooring load monitoring system, a berthing aid system and an environmental monitoring system. ·	An early warning system is provided allowing LNG-C's to leave the berth in time in case a cyclone or excessive monsoon waves approach.
Captive Jetty of L & T Hazira works with heavy load-on facility and Roll-on-Roll-off Jetty

Kakinada Deep Water Port (KDWP) was commissioned by the Government of Andhra Pradesh in November 1997 with a quay length of 610 Meters. In the year 1999 the KDWP was Privatized and Kakinada Seaports took over the operations of the Port. The Port has a Quay length of 2,500 meters for multi-product handling and 6 berths stand-alone facility for off-shore supplies for deep sea exploration. The Port handles all type of cargoes like Edible oil, Diesel, Steel, Granite, Containers, Alumina, Ammonia, Phosphoric and Sulphuric Acid Wood Pulp, Food grains, Raw Sugar, Coal, Fertilizers, CP Coke, Project cargo etc expect hazardous cargoes.

DEENDAYAL PORT IS LOCATED IN STATE OF GUJARAT TO THE INNERMOST LOCATED ON EASERN GULF OF KUTCH. PORT HAS ____ BERTHS IN STRAIGHT LINE AND 7 LIQUID CARGO BERTHS. Port has other Smaller Berths for Port Tugs and Crafts berthing as well Dry Dock. Port has approx. 13 miles long Navigational Channel starting from Fairway Buoy Called Outer Tuna Buoy (OTB).  Port has two PPP Projects and total 7 berths are under PPP Operative mode which includes Container Terminal. Port has satellite Terminal called Off-shore Oil Terminal located in Jamnagar District on Mid Central Southern Coast of Gulf Of Kutch, where 3 SBMs and two Oil Berths, One RORO LOLO and one Craft jetty is Located. Deendayal Port & Its Terminals are operational 24X7. Present Security Level is 2(TWO)

Karaikal port is a lagoon type harbour which is connected to sea by 10400mtrs long/160 mtrs wide outer approach channel. Harbour basin is well protected by 2 crescent shaped breakwaters of 600mtrs length. The breakwater extends in an easterly direction from the shore one each on North and South of approach channel. Length of inner approach channel will be 600 mtrs connected to Turning Circle  (Dia:500 mtrs). TC will have a 220mtrs opening at the southern end and tapering to 180mtrs defining 460mtrs long berth front (2 berths). Import cargo include: liquid Cargo, Coal, Coke Timber, General Cargo and export cargo will be Construction material, Clinker, Machineries etc.

MIDPL Kattupalli Port is a lagoon type harbour protected by two breakwaters with a 3 Km long & 225 metres wide outer approach channel and an inner approach channel 1.5 Km long with a Turning Circle of Diameter 580 mtrs. The Channel and berth depth is maintained at 16 mtrs.  The Port has three Berths and a Container yard. It is equipped with state-of-the-art equipment such as 8 Quay Cranes and 18 RTGCs. The terminal has an annual capacity of 1.2 million TEUs and a total handling capacity of 24.65 MMT.  A CFS is also available within the Port Complex with a closed warehouse. Apart from Container Cargo the Port handles Project, Dry, Liquid and Ro Ro Cargo also.   The Port harbour also has a Shipyard and a Modular Fabrication Facility which are operated by L&T Ship Building and L&T Hydrocarbon respectively.

Syama Prasad Mookerjee Port, Kolkata, (SMPK) is a Major Port of India and the only  riverine port, situated on the East Coast of India. It has TWO Dock Systems.  Kolkata Dock System (KDS) is situated on the left bank of the river Hooghly. Haldia Dock Complex (HDC) is situated on the right bank of River Hugli in position Latitude of 22°02' North and Longitude 88°06'East. In addition SMPK has 6 oil jetties at Baj Baj (13 N.Miles South of Kolkata), 04 oil jetties at Haldia, and river anchorages at Diamond Harbour , Sagar and Inner Sandheads.
The port consists of: 1) Outer anchorage 2) Jetties : 20 meter in length for Roll-on-Roll off and adjoining 30 meter in length for load on load off.

Description: Aqaba New Main Ports ( ANMP ) this port facility consist of 9 berths & it is located at the southernmost end of the coast of Aqaba City, and it is bordered on ad, grain silos and the fertilizer company factory, and on the north it is bothe south by the borders of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, and on the east by the coastal road,north by the docks of the Jordan Industrial Ports Company, This port facility consists of nine berths in addition to the basin of Aqaba Port Marine service Company which is responsible for towing & pilottage operations, in addition to the Prince Hamzah Pollution Control Center in the northern part of these nine berths, which have a total length of 2050 meters, with a draft  ranging from 13.5 meters to 14 meters, berths 1-8 are used to handle general & Bulk cargo, cars and livestock. Berth No. 9 is specialized for grain transport,all of these berths  are considered to be as one port facility & operated by Aqaba Company for Ports operation & Management

contains Two Berths ( The Oil Jetty & the LPG Jetty ) The Oil Terminal is consists of four dolphins berth , 24 meters draft and receives vessels of 406000 tons displacement/ LPG Jetty is located  North east oil jetty ,180 meters long with two loading platforms, receives LPG carriers only
Located close to the town of Aqaba City comprises of 12 berths with a total length of 2120 meters, mainly uesed for handling of general cargo,grain, phosphate and cars

The Container Terminal is located 5 Km south of the Main Port and is operated by APM Terminals . It has  3 berths with total  length of 1000 m.with adjoining  RO RO berth, the tereminal has acpacity to store 13.500 TEU on site.

Industrial Port ( JFI ) is operated by Jordan Industrial Ports Company, this port facility which  is located 18 Km south of the main port of Aqaba and consists of three berths:-  1 - the West  berth is 220 meters long , 15 meters draft , to serve vessels up to 70000 tons ,for handling ( Mainly Sulphur ) potash, and dry bulk materials. 2 - The Estern berth is 190 meters long, 11 meters of draft, recives vessels up to 40000 tons, for handling  Ammonia Gas , Chemical products ( mainly Phosphoric Acid ) & could be used for Fuel Oil  3 – the North Berth which is newly established 190 m length, 20 m draft for exporting Dry Bulk mainly fertilizers.
This Port Facility is used for tug boats which servicing ALSHEIKH SABAH LNG TERMINAL

The Freetown Port facility is located in the Eastern side of Freetown and handles vessels that collect and deliver Ro-Ro, Containers, Bulk, Break Bulk, Neo Bulk and Liquid Bulk Cargoes.

The Kissy Petrojetty facility is responsible for servicing international vessels delivering petroleum products to the various petroleum companies in Sierra Leone. A Port facility Security Plan (PFSP) has been developed and approved after a Port Security Assessment (PFSA).

This Port facility has been a disused iron ore facility since the mid 19th century. Now under the ownership of the African Minerals Limited (AML) it is in the process of being reconstructed and developed similar to its original capability with modern cargo conveyor belt equipment and technology into an operational iron ore export facility through stockpiling and conveyor belt loading of iron ore with its first ship due early September 2011. Security in general is well defined and briefed to all those assigned security duties, however those employees and contractors not with a specific security role are unfamiliar with the ISPS Code and its requirements under normal operations and during heightened security levels. This is been addressed as part of the familiarization and awareness process on site and during safety induction in order to make all aware of their responsibilities to report any activity they may perceive, or be witness to, as causing a security incident or breach thereof.

The Nitti Port is responsible for servicing vessels that collect bulk cargo, consisting of various minerals. ADPC’s commercial inauguration of Khalifa Port, and its highly successful launch of neighbouring Khalifa Industrial Zone Abu Dhabi (Kizad) in 2010, will catapult it onto the world business stage. The company is now competing with the best ports and industrial zones in the world. And, reacting prudently to the world economic downturn, it has steadily built an enviable reputation for delivering value for money, high quality service and access to customers.

EMAL is a state of the art aluminium smelter complex supplying the world with high quality metal. The advanced smelter in Al Taweelah currently uses DX Reduction Cell Technology to produce 750,000 tonnes of aluminium annually.

Khalifa Port is the new state of the art gateway to Abu Dhabi. It will handle all of Abu Dhabi’s container traffic once the container traffic transition from Mina Zayed is completed in early 2013. Khalifa Port has the first semi-automated container terminal in the region.

Located some 15 kms from Sharjah's city centre and 30 kms from Dubai, Hamriyah Port has been designed to benefit from not only a deep water access point but also from easy access to the UAE's super highway network that links all seven Emirates as well as providing connections to neighbouring Gulf States. The most important cargo handled in this port is LPG, oil and grain and other General Cargoes. Hamriyah Port is able to accommodate very large and deep drafted vessels where the length of jetties is 1145 meters with depth 14 meters. The Port currently has one LPG berth, one general oil products berth and four general cargo berth/grain berth. Primarily designed for handling grains, these berths and quays are being upgraded and the port is now capable of handling many other cargoes. Active steps are being taken to increase the length of the general cargo berth and to generally upgrade handling activities.

Description: Located on the UAE's eastern seaboard, Khorfakkan is the only natural deep water harbour in the Gulf region and being outside the sensitive "Straits of Hourmuz" has gained in popularity with many major container operators. This five berth facility with a total quay length 1495 meters is served by 14 Gantry cranes as well as a multitude of shore based handling equipment ensuring rapid vessel turnarounds to time sensitive line operators.

Al Jazeera Port Provides a complete range of maritime and shipping services. Our main activities are Dry Docking, Ship Repairing, Marine Operations, Agency Service, Cargo Handling and Warehouse Storage website: www.rakports.ae

Saqr Port provides conservancy and vessel traffic services for ships and craft using RAKMC, including the maintenance of Navigational channels, mooring, lights and provision of hydrographic, tidal and other information. Saqr Port is also the Pilotage authority within RAKMC Harbour limits  website: www.rakports.ae

RAK Khor Port is a unique city centre port. With the increasing interest in leisure activities in the Northern Emirates, the Port now provides access to a new passenger cruise terminal. This new passenger and tourist facility is able to process UAE Visa applications, and provide Immigration clearance to arriving and departing passengers. website: www.rakports.ae

Located in the heart of Sharjah city, Port Khalid is a purpose built facility consisting of 21 berths plus two oil berths able to handle a variety of shipping services and is linked to the rest of the Emirates by several multi lane super highway. The port handles a wide variety of tonnage ranging from tankers, container vessels, Ro-Ro ships, pure car carriers, reffers ships, passenger ships, heavy lift ships; jack up rigs, bulk carriers and a multitude of smaller vessels such as coaster, supply boats, tugs, barges and crew boats. Port Khalid is one of the easiest ports to enter in the Gulf with only a short approach channel.

The San Diego Captain of the Port Zone comprises the land masses and waters of Arizona; in Utah: Washington, Kane, San Juan, and Garfield Counties; in Nevada, Clark County; and in California, San Diego and Imperial Counties. The offshore boundary, which includes all ocean waters and islands contained therein, starts at the intersection of Orange-San Diego County lines (approximately 33deg 22.5 sec N. latitude) and the California Coast and proceeds seaward on a line bearing 225 degrees T to the outermost extent of the EEZ; thence proceeds southerly along the outermost extent of the EEZ to the intersection of the maritime boundary with Mexico; thence easterly, along the maritime boundary with Mexico to its intersection with the California Coast.

The Area of Responsibility (AOR) of the Captain of the Port is defined by 33CFR03.55-20 as: the land masses and waters of Wyoming within the boundaries of Sweetwater County; Utah, except for Washington, Kane, San Juan, and Garfield counties; Nevada, except for Clark County; and California, north San Luis Obispo, Kern, and San Bernardino Counties.  It also includes all ocean waters and islands contained therein of the EEZ bounded on the north by the northern boundary of the Eleventh Coast Guard District described in 33CFR03.55-1; and on the south by a line bearing 240o degrees true from the intersection of the Monterey-San Luis Obispo County lines and the California coast to the outermost extent of the EEZ; and on the west by the outermost extent of the EEZ.

The COTP PWS Zone encompasses the Port of Valdez in the north to the Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ) in the Gulf of Alaska and includes the majority of Prince William Sound (excluding areas west of W. longitude 148°26' and the community of Whittier which falls under the COTP Anchorage Zone).  Port Valdez is the United States’ northern most ice free port with road access to Alaska’s interior, Northern Canada, the U.S. Pacific Northwest, and the Pacific Rim trade routes.  Port Valdez is the southern terminus of the Trans Alaska Pipeline System (TAPS), which terminates at the Valdez Marine Terminal, located on the southern shore of Port Valdez.
